figures.caption: TABLE I: Quantitative results on two polyp segmentation benchmarks. The best three results are shown in red, green, and blue. “↑/↓” denotes that a higher/lower score is better. “Ours-R” or “Ours-P” present ResNet50 or PVTv2B3 as backbone.
TABLE II: Quantitative results on three polyp segmentation benchmarks.
Fig. 1: Visualization results predicted by existing models (i.e., CFANet [10], PolypPVT [19], and LSSNet [21]) and ASGNet.

Fig. 2: Overall framework of the proposed ASGNet method, which consists of the basic encoder, the spectrum-guided non-local perception (SNP) module, the multi-source semantic extractor (MSE), and the dense cross-layer interaction (DCI) decoder.
Fig. 3: Details of the spectrum-guided non-local perception module.
Fig. 5: Details of the dense cross-layer interaction decoder.
